provenance.method: Numbered color-band calibration grids (10 bands per placement, labeled) rendered through REAL Printful mockups, then scanline-measured to the pixel. Not estimated — printed and photographed by the manufacturer's own pipeline.
provenance.units: wu = worn units: the calibration front-photo pixel frame (1000x1000, garment-scaled). All maps are affine: wu = a * template_fraction + b, stored as [a, b].
cross_checks: front vs back physical scale after camera normalization: 1137 vs 1133 wu per template height (0.4% agreement)
sleeve label columns cut in half exactly at the outer-arm silhouette proves template x=0.5 lies on the fold
sleeve pressed-flat span 122 wu matches cosine-wrap prediction 2R for template half-width

notes.front: Template maps to wu via x=[1083,-41], y=[1137,-11.9]; only x-frac 0.273-0.721, y-frac 0.135-0.833 is visible on the worn garment (neck 150, hem 935, chest 255-745). Everything outside wraps to sides or is cut.
notes.back: Back template physical scale equals front (after camera normalization). Painted back views are x-ray (wearer-left at canvas right), so the back slice MUST be flopped or the print is mirrored on the garment — the root cause of side seams never matching.
notes.sleeves: Template x=0.5 is the outer-arm fold. Right sleeve: template x [0.5,1] faces FRONT (fold->underarm). Left sleeve: template x [0,0.5] faces FRONT (underarm->fold). No mirroring in Printful's render. Sleeve art must be drawn 13.8% larger in template px than front art to print at equal physical size (0.379 vs 0.431 wu per template px).
notes.pocket: Pocket piece is roughly the middle third of its 3000px template: visible x-frac 0.31-0.69, y-frac 0.39-0.64. Pocket template frac 0.5 sits at front template frac 0.648.
notes.hood: Coarse (wrinkled in calibration photos): front face opening shows the bottom ~15% of the hood template; middle fracs wrap over the crown to the back.
